metadata.title: Guideline on Comparability of Biotechnology-Derived Medicinal Products After a Change in the Manufacturing Process: Non-clinical and Clinical Issues
metadata.notes: No prior extraction found in output-v2, v3, or v4. Replaces CPMP/3097/02. Full extraction performed.

rules.rule_text: Demonstration of comparability after a manufacturing process change is a sequential process beginning with quality studies (limited or comprehensive) and supported, as necessary, by non-clinical, clinical and/or pharmacovigilance studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Demonstration of comparability is a sequential process, beginning with quality studies (limited or comprehensive) and supported, as necessary, by non-clinical, clinical and/or pharmacovigilance studies. If a manufacturer can provide evidence of comparability through physico-chemical and biological studies, then non-clinical or clinical studies with the post-change product are not warranted.
rules.exceptions: If comparability is demonstrated through physico-chemical and biological studies, non-clinical or clinical studies may not be required

rules.rule_text: The selection of non-clinical and clinical studies must be product-driven; the strategy for comparability testing must be chosen to best predict and detect clinically relevant differences with sufficient accuracy.
rules.verbatim_quote: The selection of non-clinical and clinical studies is product-driven, i.e. a strategy for comparability testing should be chosen that best predicts or detects clinically relevant differences with sufficient accuracy.

rules.rule_text: The need, extent and nature of non-clinical and clinical comparability studies must be determined on a case-by-case basis considering risk factors including: process complexity, nature of the change, nature and extent of physico-chemical differences, product complexity, structure-activity relationships, immunogenicity risk, mode of action, therapeutic indications, posology, and previous experience.
rules.source_section: Section 4.1 - Need for Non-clinical and Clinical Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: The need, extent and nature of non-clinical and clinical comparability studies will be determined on a case-by-case basis in consideration of various factors that may be associated with risk, such as: The process complexity, the nature of the change, the potential impact on the molecule structure and on the final product profile. The nature, and extent of differences demonstrated by the physico-chemical and quality-related biological characterisation... Relationship between the therapeutic protein and endogenous proteins and the severity of (potential) consequences for immunogenicity... Mode(s) of action: unknown or multiple modes of action complicate the evaluation of the impact of changes.

rules.rule_text: The extent of comparability studies increases if manufacturing changes are introduced at later stages of clinical development; a change after conduct of confirmatory efficacy and safety studies represents the most challenging situation requiring the most thorough comparability exercise.
rules.source_section: Section 4.1 - Need for Non-clinical and Clinical Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: The extent of the comparability studies will likely increase if manufacturing changes are introduced at the later stages of clinical development. A change after conduct of confirmatory efficacy and safety studies represents the most challenging situation.

rules.rule_text: Non-clinical studies must be comparative in nature and designed to detect differences in response between the pre- and post-change products, not just the response per se; the pre- and post-change products must be compared concurrently within the same study.
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-clinical studies should be comparative in nature and should be designed to detect differences in response between the pre- and post-change products and not just the response per se. The pre- and post-change products should be compared concurrently within the same study.

rules.rule_text: For in vivo comparability studies, the final formulation of the post-change product should preferably be used rather than the drug substance alone; ideally, the formulation used in non-clinical studies should be the same as that used in clinical trials.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2 - Non-clinical Data / In vivo Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: The final formulation of the post-change product should preferably be used rather than the drug substance alone. Ideally, the formulation used in non-clinical comparability studies should be the same as that used in clinical trials to facilitate interpretation of data. State of the art methods should be employed.

rules.rule_text: Comparative immunogenicity endpoints must be included in repeated dose toxicity studies, even though the predictive value of animal models for immunogenicity in humans is low, to aid interpretation of in vivo studies.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2 - Non-clinical Data / In vivo Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: The immune response, e.g. antibody titres, neutralising capacity, cross-reactivity. Although the predictive value of animal models for immunogenicity in humans is low, (comparative) immunogenicity endpoints should be included in repeated dose toxicity studies to aid in the interpretation of these studies.

rules.rule_text: Pharmacokinetic studies are an essential part of the clinical comparability exercise and must be comparative in nature, not merely characterizing clinical pharmacology per se.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pharmacokinetic studies are an essential part of the clinical comparability exercise. Since the aim is to demonstrate comparability and not the characterization of clinical pharmacology of the product per se, such studies should be comparative.

rules.rule_text: For clinical comparability PK studies, the selected dose should be in the steep part of the dose-response curve to detect relevant differences; if the product is planned for multiple routes of administration, it may be necessary to test all routes.
rules.verbatim_quote: The selected dose should be in the steep part of the dose-response curve, in order to detect relevant differences. The choice of the population (healthy volunteers versus patients) is primarily driven by the mode of action of the product... If the product can is planned to be administered by more than one route (e.g. s.c. and i.v.), it may become necessary to test all routes.

rules.rule_text: For PD endpoints used in comparability studies, the equivalence margin must be pre-specified and justified; endpoints must fulfil: (1) sensitive enough to detect small differences, (2) measurable with sufficient precision, and (3) clinically relevant for the target population.
rules.verbatim_quote: As a principle, an endpoint should be selected that fulfils the following requirements: (1) sensitive enough to detect small differences, (2) measurable with sufficient precision, (3) clinically relevant for the target population... The choice of marker(s) should be justified and the margin defining equivalence should be pre-specified and justified.

rules.rule_text: If no suitable PD markers exist or PD studies fail to establish comparability, a comparative equivalence clinical trial using clinical endpoints is required; studies must be randomised, double-blind, and use the highest probability of showing a difference; the equivalence margin must be pre-specified.
rules.verbatim_quote: If no suitable markers exist, or if pharmacodynamic studies fail to establish comparability clearly, a comparative equivalence clinical trial using clinical endpoints will be required... Usually, clinical studies should be randomised, double blind, to avoid bias. Possible differences in efficacy should normally be investigated in studies with the highest probability of showing a difference... The acceptable equivalence margin should be set taking into account the product release specifications, clinical relevance and statistical considerations, and be pre-specified.
rules.exceptions: If an equivalence trial design is not feasible, other designs should be explored and discussed with competent authorities

rules.rule_text: Pre-licensing safety data must be obtained in a sufficient number of patients to compare adverse effect profiles of the pre- and post-change products; the evaluation of comparative immunogenicity must be an integral part of safety evaluation.
rules.source_section: Section 4.3 - Clinical Safety and Pharmacovigilance Requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: Pre-licensing safety data should be obtained in a number of patients sufficient to compare the adverse effect profiles of the pre- and post-change product. Care should be given to compare the type, severity and frequency of the adverse reactions between the pre- and post-change product... The evaluation of comparative immunogenicity should be integral part of safety evaluation.
rules.exceptions: Further post-licensing studies may occasionally be needed, e.g. pharmaco-epidemiological studies

rules.rule_text: The applicant must provide a safety specification in the application dossier including a description of possible safety issues related to the changes in the manufacturing process.
rules.verbatim_quote: The applicant should give a safety specification in the application dossier for the medicinal product under review. This includes a description of possible safety issues related to the changes in the manufacturing process.

rules.rule_text: A risk management plan must be presented or updated within the authorisation procedure, taking into account risks identified during product development, potential risks, and any specific safety monitoring imposed on the pre-change product or product class.
rules.verbatim_quote: Within the authorisation procedure the applicant should present a risk management plan or after licensing of the product an update of the existing one in accordance with current EU legislation and pharmacovigilance guidelines. This should take into account risks identified during product development and potential risks. Any specific safety monitoring imposed to the pre-change product and/or product class should be taken into consideration in the risk management plan.

rules.rule_text: In principle, non-clinical and clinical data required for a manufacturing process change must be available before implementation of the change and marketing of the new product version; approval of the process change might be based on pharmacodynamic data with additional clinical/safety data provided after approval.
rules.source_section: Section 4.3 - Timing of the Comparability Data
rules.verbatim_quote: In principle, non-clinical and clinical data, if required, need to be available before implementation of the change in the manufacturing process, i.e. marketing the new version of the product. Depending on the product and the indication, approval of the process change might be based on pharmacodynamic data. Additional clinical/safety data, including immunogenicity data, may be provided after approval.
rules.exceptions: Additional clinical/safety data including immunogenicity may be provided post-approval depending on product and indication
